channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Chivayo throws shade at Makandiwa for declining his gifts @wicknellchivayo
item.description: HARARE — Wicknell Chivayo has revealed that gospel preacher Emmanuel Makandiwa turned down an undisclosed gift and instead offered to pray for him, a gesture the businessman said he neither wanted nor needed. “Prophet Makandiwa and his wife instead gratuitously offered to invite me to his house to pray for me to have more blessings [...] The post Chivayo throws shade at Makandiwa for declining his gifts appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Chivayo hits back at Mliswa in CAB3 gifts row, reveals ex-MP accepted $107k Ford Raptor @wicknellchivayo @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: HARARE – Controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo has turned on former Zanu PF official Temba Mliswa, accusing him of hypocrisy after Mliswa publicly condemned Chivayo’s gifts to MPs backing the Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment Bill (No. 3), which would extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s term in office by two years. In a lengthy, combative Facebook post, Chivayo [...] The post Chivayo hits back at Mliswa in CAB3 gifts row, reveals ex-MP accepted $107k Ford Raptor appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: No Child Will Come Out’: Zivhu Likens Zimbabwe to Barren Woman @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: ‘ Tinashe Sambiri| Zanu PF official Killer Zivhu tore into the government Saturday, claiming graft has spiraled beyond control and... Continue reading "No Child Will Come Out’: Zivhu Likens Zimbabwe to Barren Woman" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: What’s Wrong With Tawanda Chirewa Photo Op With Mnangagwa?
item.description: Tinashe Sambiri| Warriors star Tawanda Chirewa posted a photo of himself with President Emmerson Mnangagwa on Friday, sparking a wave... Continue reading "What’s Wrong With Tawanda Chirewa Photo Op With Mnangagwa?" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Deadly ngozi of man who was beaten to death in 1992 wipes Masamvu’s wealth... “I have 9 wives and 67 children!” (VIDEO)
item.description: The Price of Blood: How a 30-Year-Old Ghost is Wiping Out a Zimbabwean Dynasty NYANGA – In the mist-shrouded highlands of Nyanga, where tradition runs as deep as the mountain gorges, the name Daniel Masamvu was once synonymous with success. A prominent polygamist with nine wives and sixty-seven children, Masamvu stood as a titan of [...] The post Deadly ngozi of man who was beaten to death in 1992 wipes Masamvu’s wealth... “I have 9 wives and 67 children!” (VIDEO) first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Ziyambi Under Fire For Harassing Opposition MPs In Parliament
item.description: Tinashe Sambiri| By A Correspondent – Justice Minister Ziyambi Ziyambi has slammed opposition MPs for comparing the Zimbabwe Defence Forces... Continue reading "Ziyambi Under Fire For Harassing Opposition MPs In Parliament" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Disaster after enjoying unprotected lula lula: Bulawayo teenager gives birth to twins, burns them and dumps their remains at dumping site
item.description: Bulawayo – A harrowing incident has cast a sombre shadow over the Makokoba community in Bulawayo, as a 19-year-old woman, Praise Chibwanda, faces charges following the discovery of her newborn twins, allegedly burnt and abandoned at a refuse site. The tragic event has brought to the fore the escalating crisis of baby dumping in Zimbabwe, [...] The post Disaster after enjoying unprotected lula lula: Bulawayo teenager gives birth to twins, burns them and dumps their remains at dumping site first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zivhu Slams ‘Point of No Return’ Corruption in Govt @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Tinashe Sambiri| Outspoken Zanu PF official Killer Zivhu issued a blistering statement Saturday, alleging that corruption in government has become... Continue reading "Zivhu Slams ‘Point of No Return’ Corruption in Govt" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Chivayo fires back at Mliswa over CAB3 gift controversy @bulawayo24 @wicknellchivayo @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo has launched a blistering attack on former Zanu PF provincial chairman and ex-legislator Temba Mliswa, accusing him of hypocrisy after he criticised gifts aw... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zanu PF bigwigs think twice over CAB3 as Tshabangu ‘checkmates’ them... US$10,000 Bribes Exposed as Parliament Delays Vote @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Bribes, Betrayal, and the Battle for Zimbabwe’s Soul: The Dark Underbelly of CAB3 HARARE – The hallowed halls of Zimbabwe’s Parliament, usually a theatre of rehearsed rhetoric and partisan posturing, have been transformed into a marketplace of a far more sinister kind. As the nation teeters on the edge of a constitutional precipice, explosive allegations [...] The post Zanu PF bigwigs think twice over CAB3 as Tshabangu ‘checkmates’ them... US$10,000 Bribes Exposed as Parliament Delays Vote first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

Zimbabwe women settle for silver

The post Zimbabwe men’s volleyball claim CAVB Zone VI gold to Olympic dream alive first appeared on Three Men On a Boat.

item.title: General Chiwenga’s Civil War Warning as Team Mnangagwa pushes for CAB3 victory: Unpacking the VP’s Dire Prophecy
item.description: Zimbabwe finds itself at a precarious crossroads, with a chilling statement attributed to a source close to Vice President Constantino Chiwenga suggesting the nation has ‘never been this close to a civil war’. This alarming pronouncement, emerging amidst the contentious Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3) and persistent rumours of a deepening rift between President [...] The post General Chiwenga’s Civil War Warning as Team Mnangagwa pushes for CAB3 victory: Unpacking the VP’s Dire Prophecy first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Chaos in ZRP as Sergeant sues his boss and 5 other police officers for ‘messing up with him’
item.description: ZRP Cop VS 6 BOSSES: The Internal War Tearing Masvingo’s Police Force Apart MASVINGO – In the high-stakes world of Zimbabwean law enforcement, the thin blue line is usually expected to hold firm against the rising tide of crime. But in the corridors of the Masvingo Magistrate Courts, that line has not just thinned; it [...] The post Chaos in ZRP as Sergeant sues his boss and 5 other police officers for ‘messing up with him’ first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Tungwarara Seeks Truce with Matinyarare Through Dialogue and Vehicle Gift @263Chat
item.description: Presidential adviser Paul Tungwarara has offered a luxury vehicle to former government critic Rutendo Matinyarare as part of efforts to ease tensions following months of public criticism directed at President Emmerson Mnangagwa. The unusual gesture, which includes the offer of a Toyota Land Cruiser 300 [...] The post Tungwarara Seeks Truce with Matinyarare Through Dialogue and Vehicle Gift appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Former FIFA president Blatter once backed Zimbabwe’s World Cup dream: Ex-minister Mzembi
item.description: FORMER Tourism and Hospitality minister Walter Mzembi has revealed that Zimbabwe once pursued a bid to co-host the FIFA World Cup, claiming he secured the support of then-FIFA president Sepp Blatter for an exploratory campaign. In an X post, Mzembi said that while in government in 2010 he initiated talks with FIFA over a joint [...] The post Former FIFA president Blatter once backed Zimbabwe’s World Cup dream: Ex-minister Mzembi appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo

item.dc:creator: The Herald ZW

item.title: Pardon Mapondera: gone but not forgotten at Venice
item.description: The fifth and final piece in our series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Pardon Mapondera, the artist whose practice the room finally arranges itself around, treats discarded plastic and glass jars not as ecological signal but as ritual matter, and produces, in the process, the most quietly devotional installation in the Biennale this year. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Gideon Gomo: from stone to bronze, the ancestral algorithm at Venice
item.description: The fourth in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Gideon Gomo’s first sustained body of bronze work, paired with discarded vehicle number plates and Zimbabwean springstone, is the move of a sculptor at full maturity rethinking the material vocabulary of his country’s most internationally legible art form. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Shumba Maasai in Venice
item.description: At the 61st Biennale, the Zimbabwean artist’s day-long procession through the city’s campi, performed under the borrowed authority of a Murehwa chief, restages the social-sculpture lineage of Joseph Beuys for a contemporary African discursive practice. #Zimbabwe #Culture #ZimbabweCulture #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Franklyn Dzingai: family albums and the neural commons at Venice
item.description: The third in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Franklyn Dzingai’s reductive cardboard prints, built from the family album, the magazine clipping, the back-room studio photographs of mid-century Zimbabwe, turn out, on close looking, to be the most precisely engineered work in the room. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Felix Shumba: charcoal and the practice of disappearance at Venice
item.description: The second in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Felix Shumba’s six-metre charcoal wall and his Telephone Reader series anchor a body of work that argues drawing is not the act of capturing the seen, but the act of staying present to what is on its way out of sight. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Eva Raath: a slow archive of stitched memory at Venice
item.description: In the first of five long-form pieces on the Pavilion of Zimbabwe at #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Isn’t it lovely: a week in Venice with the Pavilion of Zimbabwe
item.description: A letter from Venice, written in the wake of the opening of Zimbabwe’s #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Valentine Eluwasi

item.description: Zimbabwe – a tale of two African stock exchanges — via African Capital Markets News #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
